summary: The test suite covers all stated requirements thoroughly: glob matching, multiple labels per file, priority ordering, exclusive/non-exclusive modes, mocked PR scenarios, and error handling are all represented. Class-based organization with descriptive names and docstrings is clean and readable. However, rigor has notable gaps: the '?' wildcard is never tested positively; plain single-level patterns like '*.py' (non-'**' fnmatch paths) have no dedicated coverage; tie-breaking when two rules share the same maximum priority in exclusive mode is untested; negative or zero (default) priorities are not exercised; root-level files (no directory separator) are absent; and special characters in paths or labels are not probed. The run_tests.py file duplicates the entire pytest suite verbatim, creating a maintenance burden and suggesting the tests were written to work around tooling constraints rather than as a clean design decision. Overall this is a solid suite that would catch most regressions, but the missing edge cases leave meaningful blind spots in the glob and priority logic.
